text: The skin incision is made and sub-platysmal flaps are raised.
The clavicular head of the stenocleidomastoid muscle is shown.
This is retracted medially to expose the carotid sheath.
The jugular vein is exposed, dissected,
and looped.
Next the common carotid artery is dissected in a similar fashion and looped.
Further mobilization of the internal jugular vein is carried out to later facilitate tunneling of the bypass graft.
The scalene fat pad is bluntly dissected to expose the subclavian artery.
The brachial plexus is seen laterally and the subclavian artery is eventually exposed and looped
An arteriodomy is made in the subclavian artery.
And the anastomosis between the graft and the subclavian artery is begun.
I start off with a parachute technique as shown.
The anastomosis is completed and the graft is tunneled underneath the jugular vein towards the carotid artery.
The graft is trimmed.
The carotid artery is clamped and opened.
The other end of the graft is now anastomosed to the carotid artery as shown.
The anastomosis is completed.
The clamps are released and flow is established in the graft.
The jugular vein is seen overlying the bypass graft.
Hemostasis is satisfactory and the incision is closed in layers.
